title: M06 Mastery Check — Async JavaScript
description: Quick applied check. No looking things up. Answer from memory.

questions.prompt: What prints first: a Promise.then callback or a setTimeout(0) callback? Why?
questions.answer_key: Promise.then — it's a microtask. Microtasks drain after each synchronous task, before any macrotask (setTimeout).

questions.prompt: Fix: items.forEach(async item => { await save(item); }) — processing should be sequential.
questions.answer_key: for (const item of items) { await save(item); } — for...of respects await. forEach ignores returned Promises.

questions.prompt: An async function that returns 42 actually returns Promise<number>.
questions.explanation: async functions always return a Promise. The 42 is wrapped: Promise.resolve(42).

questions.prompt: You need to fetch user data and user settings at the same time. Write the call.
